title: Longest Non-Overlapping Substring with K Swaps

statement.markdown: Given a string S and an integer K, find the length of the longest non-overlapping substring with at most K swaps. For example, if the input is S = 'abcba' and K = 2, the longest non-overlapping substring with at most 2 swaps is 'abc' with a length of 3. If there are multiple such substrings, return the length of the longest one.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be in the format: S (string), K (int)
statement.outputFormat: Output will be an integer representing the length of the longest non-overlapping substring with at most K swaps
statement.notes: Note that the input string S may contain duplicate characters.

examples.explanation: The longest non-overlapping substring with at most 2 swaps is 'abc' with a length of 3
The longest non-overlapping substring with at most 1 swap is 'ab' with a length of 2

hints.text: Consider using a two-pointer technique to keep track of the start and end of the current substring

editorial.title: Editorial: Longest Non-Overlapping Substring with K Swaps
editorial.intuition: The intuition is to use a two-pointer technique to keep track of the start and end of the current substring and a sliding window approach to find the longest non-overlapping substring with at most K swaps.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would be to generate all possible substrings and check if there are at most K swaps in each substring.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use a two-pointer technique and a sliding window approach to efficiently find the longest non-overlapping substring with at most K swaps.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a two-pointer technique and a sliding window approach to keep track of the start and end of the current substring and efficiently find the longest non-overlapping substring with at most K swaps.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is based on the fact that the two-pointer technique and the sliding window approach ensure that the current substring is always the longest non-overlapping substring with at most K swaps.
editorial.dryRun: Let's consider the example S = 'abcba' and K = 2. The initial substring is 'a'. We move the end pointer to 'b' and find that there are 1 swaps. We move the start pointer to 'c' and find that there are 1 swaps. We move the end pointer to 'b' and find that there are 1 swaps. We move the start pointer to 'a' and find that there are 2 swaps. The current substring 'abc' has a length of 3 and is the longest non-overlapping substring with at most 2 swaps.
commonMistakes: Incorrect use of the two-pointer technique
Incorrect use of the sliding window approach
